title: Kohautia azurea
intro: Kohautia azurea is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues. Bioactive constituents of Kohautia azurea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to be responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. Traditionally, Kohautia azurea is used to prepare herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including infections, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Kohautia azurea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Kohautia, which is a group of flowering plants native to Africa. Kohautia azurea is further classified as a member of the tribe Rubieae, a large group of flowering plants within the Rubiaceae family. The plant's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other flowering plants and its unique characteristics. Kohautia azurea's classification is a key aspect of understanding its biology and ecology.
